mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates tightening labor markets, boosting corporate earnings visibility and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in UNRATE decline scenarios fall below 0.5% or fail to outperform baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in CPI inflation and rising Fed funds rate expectations create a 'tightening paradox' that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in this regime exceed 1.0% or show positive Rank IC > 0.05 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) during expansionary demand phases signal increased consumer wealth and business investment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in HOUST upturns are negative or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105.0 signals stronger real economic activity, boosting corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in INDPRO upturns are below 0.2% or fail to outperform baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent below-target inflation (CPIAUCSL < 1.5%) triggers central bank accommodation, lowering real rates and boosting equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in CPIAUCSL low regimes are negative or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals robust labor market dynamics, increasing consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in this interaction regime are negative or show Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) during late-cycle demand phases indicates sustained economic momentum, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in RSAFS upturns are negative or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.
